reasoning: The challenge has solid production-quality code structure and a plausible business scenario, but the defenses are thin — a single-pass keyword stripper is the only real barrier, and the API auth header is openly exposed in a JS comment on the inventory page. The recursive keyword bypass (SELSELECTECT) is a well-documented CTF trope requiring no novel thinking. Calibrated difficulty of 0.85 is honest; the 1.5 target is overshot since each step (guest login, view source, keyword doubling) is individually trivial. Decent scaffolding, but needs deeper layered defenses to justify an intermediate rating.
